methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pivotal is a growth-stage transportation company founded by Marcus Leng and backed by Page. The broader transportation sector comprises five identifiable private peers—Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—with no public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The private comp universe lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers meeting minimum clustering thresholds (zero same-stage matches identified; one adjacent-stage match; zero similar-tier matches). A cross-sector median approach would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of transportation subsegments represented in the peer set. Consequently, traditional triangulation cannot be reliably executed with the available data.

Without sufficient comparable company metrics, revenue multiples, or precedent transaction data specific to Pivotal's stage and competitive position within transportation, a quantitative valuation range cannot be defensibly supported. Any valuation conclusion would rest on unmeasured assumptions about Pivotal's relative capital efficiency, market timing, and technology differentiation relative to the five identified peers. Critical gaps include: comparable companies' growth rates, EBITDA or revenue figures, and transaction valuations for same-stage players. Prospective analysis would benefit from business model clarity, addressable market definition, and explicit articulation of Pivotal's technical and commercial differentiation versus the peer set.
